title: Project Overview
description: Start here. The README frames the project: a TypeScript monorepo migrating the Long Island CW Club Morse trainer from knockout.js to React, with a shared engine, a web app, and a native iOS app that can play Morse through the lock screen.

title: The Shared Core Engine
description: All Morse logic lives in @morsebrowser/core, framework-agnostic so web and mobile share one source of truth. This barrel re-exports the engine, audio, lessons, and settings modules; the types package defines the shared contracts.

title: The Morse Map
description: The character-to-dits-and-dahs translation table — the foundation every other engine module builds on when turning text into Morse symbols.

title: Timing Engine
description: Computes precise dit/dah/gap durations from WPM, Farnsworth, and Koch settings. Accurate timing is a hard requirement — this module produces the schedule the audio layer plays.

title: The Audio Scheduler
description: The heart of the app: it pre-schedules every tone on the AudioContext clock with zero setTimeout, so Morse stays sample-accurate even under load. Understand this to understand why the migration exists.

title: Lessons & Settings
description: lessonLoader assembles Koch lessons and word lists; settingsManager persists user preferences (WPM, frequency, etc.) via cookies. Together they feed the engine its inputs.

title: Web App Bootstrap
description: How the web app boots: main.tsx mounts React, App.tsx assembles the Bootstrap UI pixel-matched to the original fork.

title: Web Playback Flow
description: State and playback wiring on web: MorseAppContext holds app state, useMorsePlayback drives the engine scheduler, and PlaybackControls is the user-facing transport.

title: Mobile App & Native Audio
description: The Expo/React Native iOS app. _layout.tsx is the expo-router entry; audioSession.ts configures the native audio session (configureAudioSession) that enables true background / lock-screen playback the browser cannot do.

title: Mobile Playback Flow
description: The mobile counterpart to step 8: MorsePlaybackContext and useMorsePlayback reuse the same core scheduler, with playbackOrchestration coordinating loops and seeks.

title: Supporting Infrastructure
description: A small Cloudflare Worker proxies RSS feeds for the apps, and the deploy workflow ships the web build. The leanest edge of the system.
